evidence: RoleManagementModal and RolesSection contain fully duplicated RoleData interface, togglePermission function, and PermissionToggles component — identical logic in both files with minor styling differences

ChatProvider.refreshChannels, refreshMembers, refreshAgents follow an identical fetch-parse-setState-updateServerDataById pattern repeated 3 times with the same structural shape, differing only in URL path and field name

ChatPanel component (~516 lines) mixes panel windowing logic (drag, resize, maximize, z-index management) with chat domain logic (messages, typing, streaming status, mentions) — two distinct responsibilities in one body
useChannel hook (~920 lines) registers 14+ channel event handlers inline within a single useEffect, each with the same mounted-guard and type-assertion pattern, creating a monolithic effect body
Three identical copies of condition-based-waiting-example.ts exist under .claude/, .openhands/, and .pi/ skill directories — 158 LOC duplicated verbatim across all three
ProfileSettingsModal manages 13 separate useState calls for avatar, profile, theme, and password sections — four logically distinct feature areas colocated in one component

strengths: Component prop interfaces are well-defined and focused — MessageInputProps, ChatPanelProps, and BYOKFormProps each have clear, minimal surface areas
agent-factory.ts demonstrates good extraction: shared agent creation logic used by both UI and CLI bootstrap routes, with clean CreateAgentOptions/CreateAgentResult interfaces
Channel event handling in useChannel, while large, follows a consistent pattern for each event type, making it predictable to extend
Modal components follow a consistent isOpen/onClose pattern across the codebase, establishing a recognizable convention
design_coherence.issue_character: The primary pattern is responsibility accumulation in key components (ChatPanel, useChannel, ChatProvider) and verbatim duplication of role management UI across two separate file locations.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase has clear conventions and some well-extracted shared logic (agent-factory), but suffers from significant structural duplication (roles UI duplicated across RoleManagementModal and RolesSection with identical helper functions) and responsibility overloading in central components. ChatPanel conflates windowing mechanics with chat domain logic, and useChannel's single 600-line useEffect registers all event handlers monolithically. These are not cosmetic issues — they increase change friction and divergence risk. The score reflects a codebase that works and has some good patterns, but has accumulated structural debt in its most central files.

issues.summary: RoleManagementModal and RolesSection duplicate RoleData, togglePermission, and PermissionToggles verbatim
evidence: Both files define identical RoleData interface (lines 15-23 in role-management-modal, lines 14-22 in roles-section)
Both files define identical togglePermission(current, bit) function
Both files define identical PermissionToggles component with the same PERMISSION_INFO.map rendering logic
CRUD operations (handleCreate, handleEdit, handleDelete) follow the same structure with only minor differences in error handling style
issues.suggestion: Extract RoleData, togglePermission, and PermissionToggles into a shared module (e.g., packages/web/components/roles/shared.tsx). Both RoleManagementModal and RolesSection can import from it, eliminating ~80 lines of duplication and ensuring permission toggle behavior stays consistent.

issues.summary: ChatPanel mixes panel windowing mechanics (drag, resize, z-index) with chat domain logic in a single 516-line component
evidence: Lines 78-100 define dragRef and resizeRef for windowing, lines 178-206 implement mouse-down handlers for drag and resize, lines 221-279 attach global mousemove/mouseup listeners for panel manipulation
Lines 40-59 set up chat-specific hooks (useChannel, useChatContext), lines 281-307 compute mention options from server data, lines 309-317 manage streaming state
These are two independent responsibilities: one is a generic draggable/resizable panel shell, the other is a chat channel view
issues.suggestion: Extract the draggable/resizable panel shell into a generic PanelWindow component that accepts children. ChatPanel would then only contain the chat-specific logic (messages, typing, mentions, streaming status) and be wrapped by PanelWindow for windowing behavior.

issues.summary: useChannel registers 14+ channel event handlers inside a single ~600-line useEffect, creating a monolithic setup block
evidence: The main useEffect (lines 207-793) registers handlers for: message_new, sync_response, history_response, agent_trigger_skipped, user_typing, stream_start, stream_token, stream_complete, stream_error, stream_thinking, stream_tool_call, stream_tool_result, typed_message, reaction_update, stream_checkpoint, charter_status, message_edited, message_deleted
Each handler follows the same pattern: mounted guard, type assertion of raw payload, then state update — but all are inlined in one effect body
issues.suggestion: Group related event handlers into named setup functions (e.g., setupStreamingHandlers, setupTypingHandlers, setupEditDeleteHandlers) called from within the effect. This preserves the single join/cleanup lifecycle while making the effect body scannable and each handler group independently reviewable.

issues.summary: condition-based-waiting-example.ts is duplicated verbatim across three AI-agent skill directories
evidence: All three files are byte-identical (158 LOC each), confirmed via diff
Each is orphaned with zero importers — they serve as reference documentation for AI agents, not runtime code
issues.suggestion: Maintain a single canonical copy (e.g., under .claude/skills/) and either symlink or reference it from other agent config directories. Alternatively, if each agent tool requires its own copy, add a comment or script to keep them synchronized.

issues.summary: ChatProvider has three structurally identical refresh functions (refreshChannels, refreshMembers, refreshAgents) with duplicated serverDataById merge logic
evidence: refreshChannels (lines 162-190), refreshMembers (lines 192-215), refreshAgents (lines 217-242) each follow the same pattern: guard on serverId, fetch, parse JSON, set local state, merge into serverDataById preserving other fields
The serverDataById merge in each function is a copy-paste with one field name changed: channels/members/agents. If a fourth field is added, a fourth copy must be made
issues.suggestion: Extract a generic refreshServerData helper that accepts the URL suffix, response field name, and local setter, then applies the common serverDataById merge pattern. This consolidates the repeated merge logic into one place.

issues.summary: ProfileSettingsModal manages 13 useState hooks spanning four unrelated feature areas (avatar, profile, theme, password) in one component
evidence: Lines 28-43 declare 13 useState calls: displayName, email, avatarUrl, avatarUrlInput, currentPassword, newPassword, confirmPassword, saving, savingPassword, uploading, error, passwordError, success, passwordSuccess

The component handles avatar upload/URL/remove (lines 68-155), profile save (lines 157-178), theme toggle (lines 180-193), and password change (lines 195-230) as four independent workflows sharing only the modal shell
issues.suggestion: Extract each section (AvatarSection, PasswordSection) into sub-components that own their own state. The ProfileSettingsModal becomes a thin shell composing these sections, reducing cognitive load from 13 interwoven state variables to a few per sub-component.

root_causes: Feature-driven development without periodic consolidation: roles UI was built in two contexts (modal and settings page) without extracting shared components
useChannel and ChatPanel grew organically as streaming, tools, charters, and reactions were added across multiple tasks, without periodic responsibility decomposition
likely_symptoms: profile_modal_state_sprawl — likely a symptom of the same incremental-feature-addition pattern that caused chat_panel_mixed_concerns
